title: Pulsatile Abdominal Mass - Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
chief_complaint: 72-year-old male smoker with pulsatile abdominal mass found on routine examination. Asymptomatic. Ultrasound shows 6.2cm infrarenal AAA. History of hypertension and COPD.

physical_exam.abdomen: Pulsatile, expansile mass in epigastrium, nontender

hidden_data.ct_angiogram: Infrarenal AAA 6.2cm, suitable neck 2cm, no iliac involvement

correct_diagnosis: Large Infrarenal Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm (6.2cm) - Elective Repair Indicated
key_learning_points: AAA screening: one-time US for men 65-75 with smoking history
EVAR preferred for high-risk surgical candidates
Open repair for young/low-risk patients (more durable)

patient_responses.symptoms: No pain, found on routine checkup

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! 6.2cm AAA needs elective repair.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a large AAA. Size >5.5cm = repair indicated to prevent rupture.
feedback.attending_note: AAA 6.2cm: Elective repair. EVAR if anatomy suitable and high surgical risk (COPD). Open repair if low risk (more durable). Smoking cessation. Control HTN. Annual surveillance of remaining aorta.
